title: EIP-7495: SSZ ProgressiveContainer
description: Introduces a new SSZ type called ProgressiveContainer that provides forward-compatible Merkleization for containers. Each field retains a stable generalized index (gindex) even when newer container versions add or remove fields, preventing Merkle proof breakage across fork boundaries. Serialization remains compact, with absent fields consuming no space.
author: Etan Kissling (@etan-status), Cayman (@wemeetagain)
laymanDescription: Today, when Ethereum upgrades change the data structures used by the consensus layer, existing Merkle proof verifiers (like light clients and smart contracts) can break and need costly updates. ProgressiveContainer is a new data format that keeps proof paths stable across upgrades, so verifiers keep working without changes.

endUsers.description: No direct impact. End users benefit indirectly through more resilient light clients and bridges that no longer break when consensus data structures change.

appDevs.description: Smart contracts verifying Merkle proofs against beacon state (e.g., EIP-4788 consumers) gain stable gindices, reducing maintenance when forks add or remove fields.

walletDevs.description: Minimal direct impact. Wallets relying on light client proofs benefit from continued validity of proofs across forks without wallet-side updates.

toolingInfra.description: SSZ libraries and tooling must implement the new ProgressiveContainer type, including its Merkleization with active_fields mix-in and progressive tree structure.

layer2s.description: Bridges and L2s verifying beacon chain Merkle proofs on-chain benefit from stable gindices, reducing the need for verifier contract upgrades at each fork.

stakersNodes.description: No operational changes for stakers. Validators and node operators benefit from a smoother upgrade path as container schemas evolve across forks.

clClients.description: Consensus clients must implement the new ProgressiveContainer SSZ type, update Merkleization logic for progressive trees, and handle the active_fields bitvector mix-in.

elClients.description: No direct changes required. EL clients are unaffected since ProgressiveContainer applies to consensus-layer SSZ types only.

benefits: Preserves stable Merkle proof paths across forks, preventing verifier breakage.
Reduces upgrade burden for on-chain light clients and smart contract verifiers.
Enables compact serialization where absent fields consume no space.
Allows safe field removal with active_fields distinguishing absent from zero values.
tradeoffs: Adds implementation complexity to SSZ libraries for the new container type.
Field count limited to 256 including deprecated fields, though sufficient in practice.

improveUX.description: Stable Merkle proofs reduce the need for costly verifier upgrades at each fork, improving the experience for light client and bridge developers.
